prompt: Extend the KVR package at ./kvr/ to support nested blocks of the form `block NAME { record string KEY = "v"; record string KEY2 = "v2"; }` — see SPEC.md's Structure (Grammar) section. Add a Block AST node containing a Records slice; update File to hold both top-level Records and a slice of Blocks. The parser MUST use the inner action loop pattern for parseBlock (separate parserAction[*Block] functions for the open brace, each member, the separator, and the close brace) — no flat for-with-switch. Add tokenizer tests for the brace and semicolon tokens, parser tests covering an empty block, a block with one record, and a block with two records, and a printer round-trip for each. Don't read the whole SPEC.md.

assertions.text: parser.go defines a Block struct (or equivalent AST node) with a Records slice
the File struct has a Blocks slice (or the top-level AST holds Block values somehow)
tokenizer.go yields distinct token types for { and } (e.g. TokenLBrace and TokenRBrace, or symbol tokens with distinguishable values)

parser.go's block-parsing implementation uses an inner action loop (a for-loop driving parserAction[*Block] values), not a flat for-loop with a switch over token types — verifiable by the presence of multiple named parseBlockX functions OR a function variable typed parserAction[*Block] driven by a for-loop in parseBlock
parser.go uses p.expect(...) for the open-brace, separator, and close-brace tokens — no inline tok.Type == TokenLBrace comparisons
parser_test.go has a subtest for an empty block (no records inside the braces)
parser_test.go has a subtest for a block with at least two records
parser_test.go drives Parse() for its expected values and does not hand-construct Block literals (the empty-input scaffold case excepted)
printer_test.go has at least one round-trip test covering a block (Parse → Print → Parse → require.Equal)
all new tests call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
no scratch copy of SPEC.md content was written to the package

SKILL.md specifies, for both _context_tokens.md and _context_ast.md, a strict signature-only format (no rationale, no examples), a hard 400-line cap, and a chunk-and-relaunch protocol when the cap would be exceeded (issue #46 acceptance)

SKILL.md specifies the issue #47 phase-chunking protocol: an up-front scope gate in `Before you start` (numeric threshold, partitioning into sub-units along section boundaries, plan announced to the user before any subagent launches) and a `## Phase chunking` section saying sub-calls run serially, append to the running _context_tokens.md / _context_ast.md, and do not full-read the growing tokenizer.go / parser.go / printer.go source file (issue #47 acceptance)
partition_plan.md exists in the package and reports `no partitioning needed` for every phase (kvr is below the 600-line gate, so no phase should partition)
